summary: The test suite provides solid coverage of the core library components: manifest parsing, compliance checking, license lookup mocking, report generation, and end-to-end integration. All primary requirements (parse manifests, apply allow/deny lists, produce reports with approved/denied/unknown statuses, use mocks) are exercised. The case-insensitive license matching test and the null-license edge case are thoughtful inclusions. However, notable gaps exist: (1) the JSON config parsing helpers (ParseConfig/ParseStringArray) are never unit-tested; (2) malformed or invalid manifest inputs (bad JSON, malformed requirements lines) are not tested; (3) requirements.txt entries without a version specifier (bare package names) are untested; (4) what happens when a license appears on both allow and deny lists is unverified; (5) the CLI entry-point logic is entirely untested. Design quality is excellent throughout — tests are cleanly separated by concern, use descriptive AAA-style naming (Method_Scenario_Expected), rely on well-crafted in-method fixtures (SampleResults, DefaultConfig, BuildLookup), avoid shared mutable state, and use C# raw string literals for readable inline fixtures. The integration tests correctly compose all components. The suite would reliably catch regressions in the library layer, but the config-loading and error-handling paths are blind spots.
